notes_md:
**tức là** = “that is,” “which means,” or “in other words.” It introduces a clarification or a simple conclusion.

It connects two clauses, where the second part explains or restates the first in clearer terms.  

It’s very close to **nghĩa là** — you can often swap them, but *tức là* feels slightly more matter-of-fact or logical.

Use it when you want to spell something out clearly, not to add new information, just to explain what you already said.

content.sentence: Cô ấy rất thông minh, ___ cô ấy học giỏi.

translation.en: She is very smart, that is why she studies well.

example.es: Hoy es sábado, lo que significa que no tengo que trabajar.
example.vi: Hôm nay là thứ Bảy, tức là tôi không phải đi làm.
example.en: Today is Saturday, which means I don't have to work.

translation.en: She is very smart, that is why she studies well.

content.sentence: Cô ấy rất thông minh, ___ cô ấy học giỏi.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I was born in 1990, that is I am 34 years old this year.
Tomorrow I have a day off, that is I can hang out with you.
It's raining, that is I have to bring a raincoat when going out.
exampleSentences: Anh sinh năm 1990, tức là năm nay anh 34 tuổi.
Ngày mai chị nghỉ làm, tức là chị có thể đi chơi với em.
Trời mưa, tức là em phải mang theo áo mưa khi ra ngoài.
